Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 118 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Intuitions for Tranformer Circuits 41 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. How to Attract AI Bots to Your Open Source Project 114 upvotes, 16 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 176 upvotes, 40 comments
  5. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 334 upvotes, 89 comments
  6. 25 Years of Eggs 269 upvotes, 73 comments
  7. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 318 upvotes, 92 comments
  8. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 150 upvotes, 43 comments
  9. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 407 upvotes, 136 comments
  10. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 333 upvotes, 112 comments
  11. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 165 upvotes, 54 comments
  12. Migrating the American Express Payment Network, Twice 61 upvotes, 16 comments
  13. "Collaboration" Is Bullshit 76 upvotes, 23 comments
  14. A Copy-Paste Bug That Broke PSpice AES-256 Encryption 26 upvotes, 5 comments
  15. I Reverse-Engineered the TiinyAI Pocket Lab from Marketing Photos 65 upvotes, 19 comments
  16. Can you get root with only a cigarette lighter? (2024) 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. LLMs predict my coffee 105 upvotes, 43 comments
  18. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 479 upvotes, 240 comments
  19. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 191 upvotes, 94 comments
  20. The future of version control 486 upvotes, 269 comments
  21. First and Lego Education Partnership Update 39 upvotes, 14 comments
  22. The way CTRL-C in Postgres CLI cancels queries is incredibly hack-y 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Why I love NixOS 270 upvotes, 175 comments
  24. They're Vibe-Coding Spam Now 70 upvotes, 41 comments
  25. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 352 upvotes, 258 comments
  26. Windows native app development is a mess 390 upvotes, 376 comments
  27. Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 71 upvotes, 61 comments
  28. You are not your job 119 upvotes, 139 comments
  29. What Young Workers Are Doing to AI-Proof Themselves 111 upvotes, 169 comments
  30. GoGoGrandparent (YC S16) is hiring Back end Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 23 March 2026. Source here.
